per_question.question: What revenue and workforce figures are given for I-TRACING, and what 2021 revenue target is stated?

per_question.question: What gender breakdowns for homelessness did the webinar follow-up provide for the US in January 2020 and for Canada's Everyone Counts 2018?
per_question.answer: In the US in January 2020, around 62% of homeless people were men or boys, 38% were women or girls, and around 1% were transgender or non-conforming. Canadian results in Everyone Counts 2018 were similar, with 62% male, 36% female, and 2% non-binary.

Canadian results in Everyone Counts 2018 were similar, with 62% male, 36% female, and 2% non-binary._ _Administrative data are very helpful but they don't tell us anything about unsheltered people, who are roughly half of the homeless population in Canada and the US, and a large majority on the West Coast of the US, where the greatest problems are now.  The number of unsheltered people in Los Angeles County is probably greater than the total number of homeless people, sheltered or unsheltered, in Canada._

per_question.ground_truth: For the US in January 2020, about 62% of homeless people were men or boys, 38% were women or girls, and about 1% were transgender or non-conforming; 44% of sheltered people and 30% of unsheltered people were women or girls. per_question.question: How did the respondents characterize mixed methods research for homelessness solutions?
per_question.answer: Rich qualitative descriptions are good for thinking about a problem but quantitative information is needed to check if ideas from rich descriptions are just anecdotes so both methods are needed. Specialization is also good so one person should not combine both methods.
contexts:
_The major problem is counting unsheltered people, since they have incentives to be hard to find.  A secondary problem, sometimes encountered, is getting cooperation from shelters._

So you need both methods.  But specialization is also good; so I don't see any reason why one person should combine both._ _Ron Kneebone: People who experience homelessness do so for a variety of reasons including poverty, health and addiction issues, domestic violence, housing discrimination, etc. Civil society organizations deal with all these issues and in so doing play important roles in addressing homelessness._

per_question.question: What were Napoleon Water Treatment Plant's two water sources and 2020 pumping/treatment volumes?
per_question.answer: The Napoleon Water Treatment Plant's two water sources are the Maumee River and the Wauseon Reservoir. In 2020, Napoleon pumped 407 million gallons of raw water to the Wauseon Reservoir and the treatment facility provided roughly 436 million gallons of clean drinking water.
contexts:
The Napoleon Water Treatment Plant has an abundant water supply from two sources. The Napoleon Water Plant draws from the Maumee River daily. Our second source is the Wauseon Reservoir. We pump daily, weather permitting to the reservoir. In 2020, Napoleon pumped 407 million gallons of raw water to the Wauseon Reservoir. The flow can be reversed and Napoleon can flow back when there are water quality issues in the river, such as non-point agricultural runoff. Our treatment facility provided roughly 436 million gallons of clean drinking water in 2020.  The City of Napoleon public water system uses surface water drawn from an intake on the Maumee River. The City of Napoleon Public Water system did not receive any Violations for the year 2020. 

## **Definitions of terms contained within this report:** 

_**AL (Action level):** The concentration of a contaminant which, if exceeded, triggers treatment or other requirements which a water system must follow._
per_question.ground_truth: The plant draws daily from the Maumee River and also uses the Wauseon Reservoir. In 2020 it pumped 407 million gallons of raw water to the reservoir and provided roughly 436 million gallons of clean drinking water.

per_question.answer: You may wish to have your water tested and flush your tap for 30 seconds to 2 minutes before using tap water for drinking or cooking.
contexts: Lead in Home Plumbing. If present, elevated levels of lead can cause serious health problems, especially for pregnant women and young children. Lead in drinking water is primarily from materials and components associated with service lines and home plumbing. We are responsible for providing high quality drinking water, but cannot control the variety of materials used in plumbing components. When your water has been sitting for several hours, you can minimize the potential for lead exposure by Water Hotline or at www.epa.gov/safewater/lead.  Infants and young children are typically more vulnerable to lead in drinking water than the general population.  It is possible that lead levels at your home may be higher than at other homes in the community as a result of materials used in your home's plumbing.  If you are concerned about elevated lead levels in your home's water, you may wish to have your water tested and flush your tap for 30 seconds to 2 minutes before using tap water. flushing your tap for 30 seconds to 2 minutes before using water for drinking or cooking. per_question.question: What main explanation for the jobs crisis does Lawrence Mishel find more persuasive than regulatory uncertainty?
per_question.answer: The more persuasive explanation for the jobs crisis is that the demand for goods and services is depressed because of the collapse of the housing and stock market bubbles, which is the financial crisis.
contexts: profitable. The new twist is that these fears are suppressing current investments and hiring, and are thus a major cause of our unemployment problem. valent of adding 1.2 million jobs, suggesting that a lot more staffing is readily available (without making permanent new hires) to produce output of goods and services if employers so desired. It is hard to believe that regulatory uncertainty is what is preventing employers from adding work hours to current employees to fulfill current profitable opportunities to sell goods or services. Something else must be going on: Customers and sales opportunities are simply not there. Job creation has finally returned as a front and center priority in Washington. The prescription for spurring job creation, however, depends on the diagnosis of the underlying problem. Policymakers are currently invoking two very different explanations for the jobs crisis. The more persuasive explanation is that the demand for goods and services is depressed because of the collapse of the housing and stock market bubbles—the financial crisis—that has led to both a deleveraging (paying off
per_question.ground_truth: Mishel argues that depressed demand caused by the collapse of the housing and stock market bubbles and the financial crisis is the more persuasive explanation. The collapse led households to deleverage, construction to crater, non-construction investment to dry up as customers disappeared, and state and local governments to cut services and jobs as tax revenues fell.

per_question.question: Why does the paper say the regulatory uncertainty argument requires an implausible view of business behavior?
per_question.answer: The regulatory uncertainty argument requires its proponents to have a particularly peculiar form of amnesia.
contexts:
It is worth mentioning that this “regulatory uncertainty” argument requires its proponents to have a particularly peculiar form of amnesia, given that the worldwide economic collapse we are now experiencing is due to a failure to sufficiently regulate financial markets. 

## **What do economic trends and employer decisions indicate? The demand-side explanation** ## **What is the flawed reasoning behind the “uncertainty” argument?** ## **What do employers say in surveys?** 

What businesses (and business economists) say in private surveys also does not support the “regulatory uncertainty” mantra one hears from the D.C.-based business trade associations.
per_question.ground_truth: The argument assumes firms have cash, profits, financing, and current profitable sales opportunities, but are refusing to hire or invest because they fear future regulations will reduce profitability. Mishel argues this is implausible and that weak customer demand better explains business behavior.

per_question.question: How does the paper use equipment and software investment trends to challenge the uncertainty story?
per_question.answer: The paper does not mention equipment and software investment trends.
contexts:
## **Investment and hiring trends inconsistent with “uncertainty” story** 

A simple review of investment and employment trends—what businesses are actually doing— reveals that employers are not behaving according to the narrative described in the uncertainty story: Employment and investment trends are what one would expect (or better) given the trends in the overall growth of the economy (i.e., the actual growth or shrinkage of gross domestic product). Other analysts, such as Jared Bernstein (2011) and Menzie Chinn (2011), also find that the unemployment/employment growth we are experiencing is very much in line with the GDP we have seen, and no new factor—such as fear of regulation—is present. 

## **Trends in weekly hours inconsistent with “uncertainty” argument** What is the logic behind the “uncertainty” argument for our poor job growth? Based on numerous media reports, we know that firms _have substantial cash on hand to invest_ (Monga, Mattiloi, and Chasan 2011) _**,**_ but that they are not using it for new hires or investments. per_question.question: What memory-management problem does the inference engine address, and how do memory tiles help?
per_question.answer: The inference engine addresses the challenge of effective memory utilization in current CPU-based inference systems, which arises from the KV cache causing inefficiencies in memory allocation and issues with internal and external CPU memory fragmentation. Memory tiles help by allowing the memory manager to divide the available CPU memory into a set of n tiles, which the inference engine then uses to efficiently allocate incoming requests and assign their tokens to specific memory tiles based on availability, optimizing memory allocation per token.
contexts: As suggested in paged attention, one of the solutions of resolve these inefficiencies is to create memory tiles which then can be utilized efficiently based on the request. So, to streamline the inference process, the memory manager within the inference engine strategically divides the available CPU memory into a set of n tiles. The memory manager To address this challenge, we introduce an inference engine designed to enhance memory utilization by partitioning available memory into a series of tiles. This engine efficiently allocates incoming requests and assigns their respective tokens to specific memory tiles based on availability. By overseeing the computation and response delivery, the inference engine optimizes memory allocation per token, significantly enhancing performance for batching multiple requests. Effective memory utilization stands out as a primary challenge in the current CPU-based inference systems. This challenge predominantly arises from the KV (Key-Value) cache, which dynamically adjusts memory utilization in response to the number of tokens [5], [6]. Consequently, the system experiences inefficiencies in memory allocation and grapples with both internal and external CPU memory fragmentation issues. Current systems reserve the required CPU memory for storing the KV cache of a
per_question.ground_truth: CPU inference wastes memory because each request reserves contiguous KV-cache space based on maximum token length, causing internal and external fragmentation. The engine divides available CPU memory into tiles, indexes them, splits each request's KV cache into smaller chunks, allocates chunks to available tiles, fills tiles across multiple requests, and releases the tiles when a request completes.

per_question.question: What are Intel AMX's two main components as described in the paper, and how are they used?
per_question.answer: The two main components of Intel AMX are Tiles and Tile Matrix Multiplication TMUL. Tiles consist of eight two-dimensional registers, each one kilobyte in size, that store large chunks of data. TMUL is an accelerator engine attached to the tiles that performs matrix-multiply computations for AI. Together, these components allow Intel AMX to store more data in each core and compute larger matrices in a single operation.
contexts:
Intel® AMX enables AI workloads to run on the CPU instead of offloading them to a discrete accelerator, providing a significant performance boost [4]. Its architecture supports BF16 and int8 data types and includes two main components: 

- Tiles: These consist of eight two-dimensional registers, each 1 kilobyte in size, that store large chunks of data. 

- Tile Matrix Multiplication (TMUL): TMUL is an accelerator engine attached to the tiles that performs matrix-multiply computations for AI. Together, these components enable Intel® AMX to store more data in each core and compute larger matrices in a single operation. Additionally, Intel® AMX is architected to be fully extensible and scalable. The inference engine utilizes tiles and TMUL for faster matrix calculations in the model architecture. per_question.question: Which corruption theories and gender concepts form the report's analytical framework?
per_question.answer: The corruption theories that form the report's analytical framework are the Principal–Agent Model, the Fraud Triangle, Need or Greed, and institutionalized corruption. The gender concepts added are discussions about a ‘fairer sex or fairer system?’, risk aversion, and differences in opportunities and networks.
contexts: This report draws on a literature review into the dynamics of, and rationales for, corruption, including the theories of the Principal–Agent Model, the Fraud Triangle, Need or Greed and institutionalized corruption. Into this mix gender is added, drawing on the discussions about a ‘fairer sex or fairer system?’, risk aversion, and differences in opportunities and networks. This analytical framework has been complemented by the findings on sextortion. The empirical section of the report builds This report was written by Jenny Grönwall and Moa Cortobius with Pilar Avello and Maria Jacobson. During her internship at SIWI, Tania Braimok contributed greatly to the literature review and took part in the field work in Johannesburg, where focus group discussions were conducted with Social Surveys Africa. Forum Syd Colombia kindly provided guidance, contacts and logistical support to Hanna Helander who carried out the focus group discussions in Bogotá, Colombia. per_question.question: What groups does the Adoplayer privacy policy discuss for personal data collection, and what examples of Personal Data does it list?
per_question.answer: The Adoplayer privacy policy discusses Website Visitors, Users, and Partners for personal data collection. Examples of Personal Data include IP address, first and last name, postal and email address, telephone number, data from social networks, areas of interest, interest in products, and information about the type of relationship that exists between the Company and Visitors, Users, and/or Partners.

## **Privacy Policy** 

Last updated: April 2018 

## **1. Introduction** 

Thank you for visiting the http://www.adoplayer.com website (the "Website"). Your privacy and the integrity of any information you provide are important to us at Adoplayer Ltd. (“Company; We”), the operator of this Website. ## **7. Data Protection Officer** 

The Company has a “Data Protection Officer” who is responsible for matters relating to privacy and data protection. This Data Protection Officer can be reached at the following address: dataprotectionofficer@adoplayer.com. 

## **8. Further Information** 

If you have any further questions regarding the data collects, or how we use it, please contact us by email at: Support@adoplayer.com. The following sections cover the specifics of each of the three groups from which Personal Data is collected: Website Visitors, Users and Partners. For the purposes of this Policy Personal Data shall mean and may comprise: IP address, first and last name, postal and email address, telephone number, data from social networks, areas of interest, interest in products, as well as information as to the type of relationship that exists between the Company and Visitors, Users and/or Partners.
per_question.ground_truth: It discusses Website Visitors, Users, and Partners. per_question.question: What visitor data and analytics practices does Adoplayer describe for website visitors and users?
per_question.answer: Adoplayer uses cookies and other tracking technologies to uniquely identify visitors and/or users' browsers and to gather information about how they interact with the website and services. This information is used for assisting visitors and/or users in navigation and assisting in registration. The company also uses collected data to communicate with visitors, users, and/or partners, customize content, show ads on other websites, and improve its website by analyzing how visitors and/or users navigate it.

## **Privacy Policy** 

Last updated: April 2018 

## **1. Introduction** 

Thank you for visiting the http://www.adoplayer.com website (the "Website"). Your privacy and the integrity of any information you provide are important to us at Adoplayer Ltd. (“Company; We”), the operator of this Website. The Website and some of our services use cookies (small text files containing a string of alphanumeric characters that we put on your computer) and other similar tracking technologies (like web beacons, tags and scripts) to uniquely identify Visitors' and/or Users' browser and to gather information about how Visitor and/or User interact with the Websites and services. We use this information for the following purposes: Assisting Visitor and/or User in navigation; Assisting in registration, ## **2.2.1 Purpose of processing personal data** 

The Company uses the collected data to communicate with Visitors, Users and/or Partners, to customize content for Visitors, Users and/or Partners, to show ads on other websites and to improve its Website by analyzing how Visitors and/or Users navigate its Website. 

## **2.2.2 Sharing personal data**
per_question.ground_truth: It may record IP addresses, use cookies, add page-view activity, process volunteered form data, retrieve publicly available social media information if provided, and gather visit data such as visitor numbers, geolocation, time on site, clicked pages, and referrers. It also uses Google Analytics to track visits, pages, and prior sites, with Google able to use data under its terms and privacy policy.

per_question.question: How does the policy describe Data Regions and processing locations for European and US accounts?
per_question.answer: A Data Region is a set of data centers in a defined geographical area where Visitor and/or User data is stored. For Visitor and/or User with accounts in the European Data Region, all Personal Data is processed in the EEA. For Visitor and/or User with accounts in the Data Regions of the United States of America (US), all Personal Data is processed solely in the respective country.
contexts:
The Company offers a number of data regions. A “Data Region” is a set of data centers located within a defined geographical area where Visitor and/or User data is stored. Personal Data is not transmitted between Data Regions. For Visitor and/or User with accounts located in European Data Region, all Personal Data is processed in the EEA. For Visitor and/or User with accounts in the Data Regions: United States of America (US), all Personal Data is processed solely in the respective country. For Visitor and/or User with accounts located in European Data Region, all processing of Personal Data is performed in accordance with privacy rights and regulations following the EU Directive 95/46/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 24 October 1995 (the Directive), and the implementations of the Directive in local legislation. From May 25th, 2018, the Directive and local legislation based on the Directive will be replaced by the Regulations (EU) 2016/679 of the European In order to provide services to its Users, the Company collects certain types of data from them. This section will describe how these two types of data are collected and used by the Company as well as geographical differences that effect this policy. 

## **2.3.2 Collection of User data**
per_question.ground_truth: A Data Region is a set of data centers in a defined geographical area where Visitor and User data is stored, and Personal Data is not transmitted between Data Regions. Accounts in the European Data Region are processed in the EEA, while accounts in US Data Regions are processed solely in the United States.

per_question.question: When is Adoplayer a Controller versus a Processor under the policy?
per_question.answer: The Company is the Processor for User data provided by the Company to Partner and the Partner will be the Controller in accordance with Directive and GDPR.

## **Privacy Policy**

## **1. Introduction**

The Company processes Personal Data both as a Processor and as a Controller, as defined in the Directive and the GDPR: 

The Company, which you as a Partner entered an agreement with, will be the Processor for User data provided by the Company to Partner and the Partner will be the Controller in accordance with Directive and GDPR. ## **7. Data Protection Officer**

per_question.ground_truth: The Company is the Processor for User data provided by the Company to a Partner, while the Partner is the Controller. For User data provided by the User to the Company, the Company is the Controller under the Directive and GDPR.

per_question.question: What does the policy say about retention, children, and the Data Protection Officer contact?
per_question.answer: The Company will not retain User's data longer than it is necessary to fulfill the purposes for which it was collected or as required by applicable laws or regulations. The Company does not collect any information from anyone under 18 years of age and will not knowingly encourage any minor to register or access features that require registration. The Data Protection Officer can be reached at dataprotectionofficer@adoplayer.com.

per_question.question: What reopening model did Marshall High School plan for August 9, 2021, and what factors shaped the plan?
per_question.answer: Marshall High School planned to return to a full in-person learning experience on August 9th, 2021. The plan was shaped by the belief that they could create an in-person educational experience that is rich, engaging, and nurturing while also prioritizing safety for students and staff.

per_question.question: What safety measures does the plan describe for masks, self-monitoring, quarantine, and arrival temperature checks?
per_question.answer: All staff and students must self-monitor symptoms and temperature before arriving. Students and staff who are ill must stay home and get tested immediately. Temperatures above 100 degrees Fahrenheit will send students home and they will not be allowed in the school. The school will have an IP-TGMIR temperature camera at the entrance for a contactless way to read body temperature. School personnel should stay away from others, especially people at higher risk for getting very sick from COVID-19.
contexts:

The School will actively encourage sick students or staff to stay home until they have recovered.

per_question.ground_truth: Ohio had lifted the mask order as of June 1, and masks would be optional when school resumed in August after being worn through the end of 2020-21. Staff and students must self-monitor symptoms and temperature before arrival, close contacts must quarantine unless recently recovered or fully vaccinated and symptom-free, and students/staff with temperatures above 100°F at arrival will be sent home using a contactless IP-TGMIR temperature camera.

Students will also be provided with supplies that will be sanitized after each use.

per_question.ground_truth: Students must complete weekly academic activities through online or offline curriculum, and assignments require 70% mastery or better to progress. Promotion requires 5 total credits and 4 core credits for 10th grade, 10 total credits and 7 core plus 3 additional core credits for 11th grade, and 15 total credits and 10 core credits for 12th grade. Attendance is documented daily in hourly increments with 4.5 hours of learning opportunities per day, and Ohio law requires automatic withdrawal after 72 consecutive hours or 16 school days without legitimate excuse or documented participation.
